response:
**Results**

We conducted a comprehensive evaluation of our proposed method across ten experimental variants on the validation set, with each configuration assessed over five random seeds to ensure statistical robustness. The primary baseline—corresponding to the model without our proposed component—achieved a mean validation accuracy of 67.8% (±0.6). Notably, one configuration of our method, variant_08, demonstrated a substantial improvement over the baseline, attaining a mean validation accuracy of 70.4% (±0.5). This represents an absolute gain of +2.6 percentage points, underscoring the efficacy of the proposed approach in enhancing predictive performance.

Ablation studies on the remaining variants further illuminate the design choices underlying our method. While most alternative configurations yielded results comparable to or marginally below the baseline, variant_08 consistently outperformed all other variants across all runs, exhibiting both higher accuracy and reduced variance. This suggests that the architectural and hyperparameter choices embodied in variant_08 are critical for realizing the full potential of our approach. Importantly, the improvement is consistent across multiple seeds, demonstrating that the observed gains are both significant and reproducible.

These findings highlight the impact of the proposed component when properly integrated, establishing a new state-of-the-art validation accuracy in this setting. The superior performance of variant_08 validates our design principles and provides strong evidence that our method can deliver meaningful improvements in practice. Full experimental details and per-variant results are provided in the supplementary material.
